design_philosophy.core_loop: Vertical scrolling shoot-and-survive loop: navigate waves of enemies, collect power-ups to upgrade main cannon and select specialty weapons, destroy fortress bosses within time limits, progress through 12 areas to destroy the System.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Dynamic difficulty scaling via Automatic Level of Difficulty Control (ALC) creates constant tension—skilled play triggers harder enemy waves while mistakes reduce difficulty, making each player's moment-to-moment decisions impact the game state.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Pilot the AFX-6502 Zanac, humanity's last hope, through overwhelming odds to penetrate and destroy the System—a boundless part-organic, part-mechanical entity threatening all life.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Zanac's core opinion is that difficulty should be personalized through AI observation rather than static settings. The game watches player behavior (firing rate, power-up collection, boss performance, life loss patterns) and adjusts enemy aggressiveness in real-time. This creates an adaptive challenge that grows with the player, refusing to stay static or controllable by traditional means.

difficulty.lives_system: Start with 3 lives; earn additional 1-ups through high point accumulation
difficulty.continues_system: Continue option allows restart from the level where all lives were lost
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based checkpointing; progress saved at each area start
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Unique adaptive AI (ALC) creates perceived agency through hidden observation: frequent shooting, power-up collection, and slow boss completion increase difficulty; losing lives, starting new levels, or destroying reconnaissance planes reduce it. Players cannot directly control difficulty but their playstyle shapes the challenge.

progression.upgrade_system: Main cannon power-ups (blue boxes) increase bullet count and speed through 8+ upgrade levels. Eight distinct specialty weapons (numbered 1-8) each with upgrade chains—player selects weapon type via power-up collection and upgrades current weapon by collecting matching numbered power-ups.
progression.unlock_mechanism: 12 sequential areas unlocked through completion. Hidden Area Select accessible via 13 soft resets allows player-chosen starting point.
replayability_hooks: Alternative ship appearance selectable via A+B in main menu
Specialty weapon variety (8 distinct types) encourages different playstyles
ALC-driven adaptive difficulty creates unique challenge curve per playthrough based on skill demonstration
Hidden sound test and area select reward player exploration
Score-based 1-up economy rewards skillful play and high-score chasing

world_building_techniques: Minimal narrative framing: opening exposition explains the System as an alien Pandora's Box containing knowledge and destruction, released by humanity's failed access attempt
Environmental variety conveys scope: players traverse planets, space stations, outer space, and defensive armadas
Hidden ending message teases larger universe: 'SEE YOU AGAIN IN GUARDIC' references sister Compile title, building IP connections
Enemy and fortress visuals (disks, turrets, capital ships) imply technical empire defending the System

boss_design.philosophy: Stationary defensive architecture requiring tactical destruction—bosses are fortresses composed entirely of ground turrets rather than mobile entities. Players must destroy all turrets within time limits to earn bonus points. Design emphasizes positional strategy and resource management (ammunition/weapon choice) over pattern memorization.
notable_bosses: Capital Ships (mini-bosses)—large enemy vessels more resistant to player weapons; bullets are repelled; color shifts from blue to gold to pale yellow with red lines as damage accumulates, providing visual feedback
Stage fortresses (primary bosses)—stationary arrays of ground turrets requiring complete destruction within time limit; one or more per stage
The System (final antagonist)—part-organic, part-mechanical entity; implied to be destructible through reaching the game's core after 12 areas

faction_indicators: Humanity vs. The System: lone human starfighter against alien construct
Implied military structure: AFX-6502 designated as 'most advanced starfighter ever produced,' suggesting organized human defense program
danger_signals: The System's defensive armada grows more aggressive as player demonstrates skill (ALC escalation)
Reconnaissance planes serve as visible markers of System's tactical awareness
Capital ships and fortress turrets represent escalating defensive layers the player must penetrate
environmental_storytelling: Visual progression through planetary surfaces, space stations, and open space conveys System's spread across multiple domains
Enemy variety (meteors, aircraft, disks, turrets, reconnaissance planes) suggests layered, adaptive defensive strategy
Mini-boss color degradation (blue→gold→pale yellow) provides visceral feedback of penetrating System's defenses
Time limits on boss destruction imply pressure from System's countermeasures and reinforcements
